lede: A look at December 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jackson Holliday and Andrew Brackman, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Killing of Yasser Abu Shabab
events.desc: Yasser Abu Shabab is assassinated near Rafah, Gaza Strip.

events.title: Jena Six
events.desc: Six black youths assault a white teenager in Jena, Louisiana.

events.title: Port of Savannah
events.desc: The MV Amazon Venture oil tanker begins leaking oil while at the port of Savannah in the United States, resulting in an oil spill of approximately 500,000 US gallons (1,900,000 L).

events.title: Kingston upon Hull
events.desc: The Hastie fire in Hull kills three schoolboys and eventually leads police to arrest Bruce George Peter Lee.

events.title: Assassinations of George Moscone and Harvey Milk
events.desc: Following the murder of Mayor George Moscone, Dianne Feinstein becomes San Francisco's first female mayor.

events.title: Franklin D. Roosevelt
events.desc: World War II: U.S. President Franklin D. Roosevelt closes down the Works Progress Administration, because of the high levels of wartime employment in the United States.

events.title: Cosmo Gordon Lang
events.desc: Cosmo Gordon Lang was enthroned as the Archbishop of Canterbury, the first bachelor to be appointed in 150 years.

events.title: Montreal Canadiens
events.desc: The Montreal Canadiens ice hockey club, the oldest surviving professional hockey franchise in the world, is founded as a charter member of the National Hockey Association.

events.title: First Matabele War
events.desc: First Matabele War: A patrol of 34 British South Africa Company soldiers is ambushed and annihilated by more than 3,000 Matabele warriors on the Shangani River in Matabeleland.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The 109 electors of the several states of the Confederate States of America unanimously elect Jefferson Davis as President and Alexander H. Stephens as Vice President.

births.name: Jesse Burkett
births.line: American baseball player, coach, and manager (died 1953)

deaths.text: Johan Halvorsen, Norwegian violinist, composer, and conductor
Cardinal Richelieu, French cardinal and politician, Chief Minister to the French Monarch

observances.text: Christian feast day: Barbara, and its related observances: Barbórka, Miners' Day in Poland
Christian feast day: Barbara, and its related observances: Eid il-Burbara, a holiday similar to Halloween in honor of Saint Barbara. (Russia, Israel, Jordan, Lebanon, Palestine, Syria, Turkey)
Christian feast day: Clement of Alexandria (Anglicanism, Eastern Catholicism)
